title: Modals of Deduction
description: Reason about certainty and possibility in the present and past — must/can't for near-certainty, might/could/may for possibility — using modal + have + past participle for past deductions: must have been, can't have seen, might have left.
sources: CEFR C1 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Martin Hewings, Advanced Grammar in Use (modals of deduction and speculation: present and past, Units 27–29)
